title: Chicken Katsu Curry
description: A crispy panko-crusted chicken thigh served over rice with a rich Japanese curry sauce.
steps:
Butterfly the chicken thighs and pound to even thickness.
Set up breading station: flour, beaten eggs, panko.
Coat chicken in flour, dip in egg, press into panko.
Deep fry at 170°C for 5-6 minutes until golden.
Sauté onion and carrot until soft, add water, bring to boil.
Add curry roux, stir until thickened.
Slice chicken, serve over rice, pour curry alongside.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: Classic Roman pasta with a silky egg and pecorino sauce, finished with crispy guanciale.
steps:
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil.
Cut guanciale into strips, cook in a cold pan on medium until crispy.
Whisk egg yolks, whole egg, and most of the pecorino together.
Cook spaghetti until al dente, reserve pasta water.
Toss hot pasta into the guanciale pan off heat.
Add egg mixture, toss vigorously, adding pasta water to loosen.
Serve with remaining pecorino and black pepper.

title: Thai Green Curry
description: A fragrant and creamy coconut curry loaded with tender chicken and Thai basil.
steps:
Heat a splash of coconut cream in a wok until it splits.
Fry green curry paste for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add chicken and stir-fry until sealed.
Pour in remaining coconut milk, bring to a simmer.
Add eggplant and bamboo shoots, cook 5 minutes.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar.
Finish with Thai basil, serve over jasmine rice.

title: Beef Bulgogi
description: Thinly sliced marinated beef, grilled to caramelised perfection with a sweet-savoury glaze.
steps:
Mix all marinade ingredients together.
Add sliced beef, marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat a grill or cast iron pan to high heat.
Cook beef in batches, getting a good char on each side.
Garnish with spring onions and sesame seeds.
Serve with steamed rice and lettuce wraps.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: A luxuriously creamy Arborio rice dish with mixed wild mushrooms and parmesan.
steps:
Sauté mushrooms in butter until golden, set aside.
In the same pan, cook onion and garlic until soft.
Add rice, toast for 1-2 minutes.
Deglaze with white wine, stir until absorbed.
Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently.
Cook for 18-20 minutes until rice is creamy and al dente.
Fold in mushrooms, parmesan, and remaining butter.
Season and serve with extra parmesan and thyme.

title: Salmon Teriyaki
description: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with a homemade teriyaki sauce, served with steamed vegetables.
steps:
Mix soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar for the teriyaki sauce.
Pat salmon dry and season with salt.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Cook salmon skin-side down for 4 minutes until crispy.
Flip, cook for 2 more minutes.
Pour teriyaki sauce into the pan, let it bubble and glaze the fish.
Serve with steamed rice and vegetables.

title: Tacos Al Pastor
description: Achiote-marinated pork on warm corn tortillas with pineapple, cilantro, and onion.
steps:
Blend achiote paste, pineapple juice, chipotles, garlic, and cumin into a smooth marinade.
Marinate pork for at least 4 hours.
Grill or pan-fry pork on high heat until charred edges form.
Chop the pork into small pieces.
Warm tortillas on a dry griddle.
Assemble with pork, diced pineapple, onion, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.

title: Miso Soup
description: A warming and delicate Japanese soup with tofu, wakame seaweed, and spring onions.
steps:
Bring dashi stock to a gentle simmer.
Rehydrate wakame in water for 5 minutes, drain.
Add tofu cubes and wakame to the stock.
Dissolve miso paste in a ladleful of hot stock, then stir back in.
Do not boil after adding miso.
Serve in bowls topped with sliced spring onions.
